question_text: A country is deciding between building a new coal power station or a wind farm. The coal station costs £500 million upfront and produces 1000 MW of reliable power. The wind farm costs £800 million upfront, produces 800 MW average power (due to variable wind), and has near-zero fuel costs. Over 20 years, coal fuel costs are £2 billion, while wind maintenance costs are £200 million. Which statement best evaluates this decision?

options.label: A
options.text: Coal is better because the upfront cost is lower

options.label: B
options.text: Wind is better overall despite higher upfront cost because total 20-year cost is lower and it has no carbon emissions

options.label: C
options.text: Coal is better because it produces more reliable power and has lower total costs

options.label: D
options.text: They are equally good because both produce electricity

explanation: This requires comparing total costs (upfront + running), considering reliability differences, and environmental impact. Wind's total cost is £1 billion vs coal's £2.5 billion over 20 years, and wind produces no greenhouse gases, making it the better long-term choice despite variable output.
